content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 507 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

September 12, 1979


Gibb R. Madsen
Superintendent-President
Hartnell College
156 Homestead Avenue
Salinas, CA 93901

Dear Mr. Madsen:

     The Fair Political Practices Commission has received
your letter dated August 16, 1979.

     You stated that an administrator on your staff did not
disclose on his Statement of Economic Interests the income he
received for directing and supervising classes taught by a
private institution located outside the area of Hartnell
College in Salinas. However, since the classes were held in
the Salinas area, you asked if he should amend his Statement
to include the income he received.

     With respect to the reporting of financial interests the
Hartnell College Conflict of Interest Code requires disclosure
only of those interests "located within or subject to the
jurisdiction of the school district or if the business entity
in which the employee has an interest is doing business or
planning to do business in the school district or has done
business during the past two years in the school district."
Jurisdiction is defined as the boundaries of your district.
The disclosure category for administrators requires the disclosure of income from any business entity in which the person
is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds
any position of management.

     Thus, if the income received by the administrator on
your staff is of the type required to be reported under his
disclosure category and if the classes were held within the

Gibb R. Madsen
September 12, 1979
Page Two


boundaries of your district as defined above an amended "Income
Schedule" must be completed.

Please contact me with any questions you may have con-
cerning this matter.

Sincerely,

Gina Todd
Documents Examiner

GT:plh

156 HOMESTEAD AVENUE
SALINAS CALIFORNIA 93901
408 758-8211

GIBB R MADSEN
SUPERINTENDENT•PRESIDENT

August 16, 1979

Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Some time ago I talked with one of your staff members concern-
ing the report filed by one of our administrators. This report
was not sent on to Sacramento. It is my understanding that all
income earned from a private institution should be reported as
income. The administrator in question is now on the teaching
staff, and in checking his report, I find that he did not in-
dicate income received from directing and supervising classes
taught by a four-year institution located out of this area.
The classes were held in the Salinas area and he was the
administrator for them. He received funds from the students,
which I assume went to the university he was working for, and
he disbursed funds to the part time instructors he had employed.
This was during the time he was employed as an Assistant Dean,
and on our list for those who must file a report of Conflict
of Interest.

Please advise if I should request that he make an amended
filing for that period of time.

Sincerely yours,

Gibb R. Madsen
Superintendent-President

GRM:mc

Hartnell College is an Equal Opportunity - Affirmative Action Employer

sections.question: You stated that an administrator on your staff did not disclose on his Statement of Economic Interests the income he received for directing and supervising classes taught by a private institution located outside the area of Hartnell college in Salinas. However, since the classes were held in the Salinas area, you asked if he should amend his Statement to include the income he received.
sections.conclusion: Thus, if the income received by the administrator on your staff is of the type required to be reported under his disclosure category and if the classes were held within the boundaries of your district as defined above an amended "Income Schedule" must be completed.
sections.question_synthetic: Must an administrator disclose income from directing and supervising classes held within the school district boundaries, even if the classes were taught by a private institution located outside the district?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An administrator must file an amended Statement of Economic Interests to disclose income from directing and supervising classes if the income is required under the administrator's disclosure category and the classes were held within the school district's jurisdictional boundaries.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (dated August 16, 1979) and FPPC response (dated September 12, 1979). Question and conclusion sections identified from the response letter. Extraction confidence is high; only minor uncertainty regarding partial sentence cut-off in conclusion section.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You stated that an administrator on your staff did not disclose on his Statement of Economic Interests the income he received for directing and supervising classes taught by a private institution located outside the area of Hartnell college in Salinas. However, since the classes were held in the Salinas area, you asked if he should amend his Statement to include the income he received.

CONCLUSION: Thus, if the income received by the administrator on your staff is of the type required to be reported under his disclosure category and if the classes were held within the boundaries of your district as defined above an amended "Income Schedule" must be completed.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Hartnell College's Superintendent-President that an administrator must disclose income from directing and supervising classes on an amended Statement of Economic Interests if the classes were held within the district boundaries, regardless of where the private institution is located.
